chunks.text: Ana Claudia Arias is Professor and EE Division Chair at UC Berkeley. Her research interests include Physical Electronics (PHY) and Power and Energy (ENE). She earned her Ph.D. in Physics from the University of Cambridge in 2001, with earlier degrees from the Federal University of Paraná in Brazil. Her office is in 501 Cory Hall, and she holds office hours by appointment.

Ruzena Bajcsy is a Professor Emerita at UC Berkeley with research interests spanning Artificial Intelligence (AI), Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO), Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR), Graphics (GR), Human-Computer Interaction (HCI), and Security (SEC). She holds Ph.D.s in Computer Science from Stanford University (1972) and Electrical Engineering from Slovak Technical University (1968).

Jessica Boles is an Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley, focusing on Power and Energy (ENE), Integrated Circuits (INC), Micro/Nano Electro Mechanical Systems (MEMS), and Physical Electronics (PHY). She earned her Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from MIT in 2022. In Spring 2026, she teaches EE 113B and EE 213B, both Power Electronics Design courses.

Serina Chang is an Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley specializing in Artificial Intelligence (AI). She completed her Ph.D. in Computer Science at Stanford University in 2024 and holds a B.A. in Computer Science from Columbia University (2019).

Constance Chang-Hasnain is a Professor Emerita at UC Berkeley with research interests in Physical Electronics (PHY). She earned her Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from UC Berkeley in 1987, along with M.S. and B.S. degrees from UC Berkeley and UC Davis, respectively.

Sarah Chasins is an Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley, researching Programming Systems (PS) and Human-Computer Interaction (HCI). She earned her Ph.D. in Computer Science from UC Berkeley in 2019 and a B.A. from Swarthmore College in 2012. In Spring 2026, she teaches CS 294-184 on building user-centered programming tools.

Jennifer Chayes is a Professor and Dean at UC Berkeley, with research interests in Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences (IDNCS), Theory (THY), and Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO). She holds a Ph.D. in Mathematical Physics from Princeton University (1983) and a B.A. from Wesleyan University (1979).

Irene Chen is a Below The Line Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley, focusing on Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO). She earned her Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from MIT in 2022 and holds degrees from Harvard University.

Victoria Coleman is a Professor at UC Berkeley with a Ph.D. in Computer Science from the University of Manchester, UK (1991). She also holds M.Sc. and B.Sc. degrees from the University of Salford, UK. Her office hours are by appointment.

Natacha Crooks is an Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley, specializing in Database Management Systems (DBMS) and Operating Systems & Networking (OSNT). She earned her Ph.D. in Distributed Systems from the University of Texas in 2019 and a B.A. from the University of Cambridge. In Spring 2026, she teaches CS 186 on database systems.
